item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Communication Test 16195 might be one of the most disturbing pieces of writing in this entire wiki, and I'm not even an arachnophobe. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meant to change and forgot…

Done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How in the name of the names is this thing Safe? It lives, requires some maintenance, may have bred some offspring. Should be Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: reminds me of 939

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been on a lot, actually, but mostly lurking or working in my sandbox. I didn't give up on the idea, but I did set it on a backburner while I worked on a tale. Glad you both like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a competent article, but vocal mimicry and human-shaped lures have been done, and it's kind of just a giant spider otherwise. :/ Didn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe this hasn't been done before, it feels a lot like a classic scip. A picture of one of its puppets would be neat to add. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, cool, thanks for the information. I'll definitely incorporate this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Long Stuff
- Long Stuff

The initial interview with the MTF captain was much, much longer (probably around three times its current size), but I decided most of it didn't add to the article. I also wrote a transcript of the MTF's body cameras that it switched to after the final line of the interview, with the MTF going into the factory to capture it, but again, I didn't feel like it added anything new to the article. But maybe jumping to the communication test is jarring.

I can certainly change or cut the pneumatic foot thrust and the steel plate. Originally this thing was a jumping spider (until SpectralDragon pointed out to me we already have a talking jumping spider on the site), and since they can jump something like 50 times their body length, I was figuring this would have to be pretty powerful, but within the normal range when taking into account its size. It would mean this thing could've jumped like half a football field. Even removing that aspect I still figured it would be pretty strong.

Dafoe was humoring it because he was trying to figure out if it was sapient or not at that point. I never really bought the interviews with skips when they call it by their SCP designation and expect to get some sort of useful response from their demands. The reasoning for the communication test — from a writing perspective — was to show that the little girl, Rachel, mentioned prior in the MTF interview, didn't really drown, but was eaten by this thing and it's repeating her last words (among other words and phrases it's picked up). I'm not sure how clear this is though, and speaks to my failure as the author, although you seemed to have gotten it. Also that the song it was singing was Ruby Tuesday, by the Rolling Stones.

I'm not sure how many people this thing has killed, but yeah, it's probably just four, or not much more than four. It started off killing birds and frogs and other stuff when it was smaller, but as it grew it depleted the surrounding area, and so started going into town at night, first killing pets, before the transients wandered into its lair, and it realized that human's were a viable food source. I figured the reason for so many lures is that it was practicing whenever it found new prey, and it discovered kids were the best lures for humans, but again, maybe this isn't too clear.

I dunno. I pictured it crooning to its babies, all the while spinning and crafting more lures, almost like a mother sitting in a rocking chair, sewing or crocheting, humming a tune while her children drift to sleep. But that same mom also eats her neighbors. Maybe that sounds more interesting than what I actually produced, but none of the other addendums I wrote really worked. I also like a lot of the SCP's that just exist, as some of the unexplained parts make it (to me, at least) even more terrifying. Looks like I may have some more work to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest, this doesn't do much for me. While this is competently written from a technical standpoint, it feels like I've seen every part of this somewhere before, not necessarily in this exact composition but a creature that imitates human speech to lure its prey in is something that has definitely been around before.

The same goes for the interviews, they are mostly well written but ultimately don't have much of an impact on me. Petrograd already mentioned most of what bothers me with them, the first log cutting out after that line builds tension - which is good - but then never resolves that. I kept looking for something I missed that would build on this but there's nothing there.

At the end of the day, this doesn't build much on the "monster that kills you" premise and leaves me kinda unsatisfied, since I kept expecting more to come while reading this. Gonna have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was a little confused about the communication test. Since Dafoe's log had just suddenly cut off after a dramatic line and he was involved in the test, I just kind of assumed it was something still happening at the factory.

I'm not sure I buy it being able to penetrate 5 cm of whatever and needing 1.5 cm of steel plate, mostly because I'm not sure why it needs to be super-duper-strong (this kinda gets into 'stock dangerous monster' territory). (Edit: Also, if it's that strong, wouldn't that be mentioned in the description?)

As for the communication test itself…. I definitely liked that it was incorporating speech from transients into the girl's, but… the back and forth didn't quite build the tension of "real or just parroting", and it ran long for that. Edit: There's also no tension if it's in the cell and no clear scientific reason for him to "humor" it, because we know there's no child there.

Also, I'm not sure why it needed so many child lures if it had only killed a few humans.

I guess no vote. It's an interesting idea and well-written, but the tone is a bit dramatic and tropey and some of the swings didn't quite land for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know how scientists anesthetize insects for surgical experiments?

Carbon dioxide.

You want to send humans in to clean this thing's enclosure, you knock it unconscious first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It and 058 should have tea sometime!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image of the SCP uttering its mindless verbalizations while stalking and destroying the speaker is more disturbing than expected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding Gunpowder here. I was super pleased to see this show up, I'd thought you'd given up on the idea. I liked it then, and I still like it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought you had left the site and given up on this idea; I was really surprised when I saw this.

Anyway, a lot better from when I last saw it. It kinda creeped me out. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah. "Book lungs" is just the name given to the primitive type of lungs that arachnids have, and describes their structure, which apparently is similar to a folded book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It actually was Euclid when I first posted, but I started to question it in my mind. I can change it back if that's the consensus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I reading it right that this thing's lungs are made of books?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Effective and creepy, +1!

I question it being labeled as Safe, as it still requires upkeep and checking-in on and maintenance and stuff; plus, it's a clever girl alright. This little lady is Euclid all the way. I'd honestly be hard-pressed to think of something alive that would fall under Safe.

I actually would like to know a bit more on where and how they corral this thing off to when it's time to clean the cell, as listed. I could see a secondary cell adjoining the main cell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Gunpowder0 and Spectral Dragon for their critique in the forums. This has been sitting in my Sandbox gathering dust for about half a year, so I figured I'd finally go ahead and post it.
